headline: Port Klang Expansion Needs RM12 Billion to Stay Competitive
context: Port Klang Authority proposed RM12 billion expansion to increase capacity from 13 million to 20 million TEUs by 2030.

cards.big: Port Klang seeks RM12 billion to expand from 13 to 20 million TEUs
cards.sub: Malaysia largest port risks losing traffic to Singapore and Tanjung Pelepas

cards.big: Port Klang market share of Strait traffic fell from 18% to 14% since 2020
cards.sub: Singapore handled 39 million TEUs in 2025 growing 3 times faster than Klang

cards.big: Westports terminal operates at 92% capacity causing 3-day vessel waits
cards.sub: Shipping lines rerouted 15% of transhipment to competing ports

cards.big: Carey Island terminal would cost RM8 billion for 7 million TEU capacity
cards.sub: Environmental concerns over mangrove destruction delayed project 2 years

cards.big: Port expansion tests whether Malaysia can compete for maritime trade without environmental cost
RM12 billion needed for Port Klang to stay competitive. Delays hand market share to Singapore and Tanjung Pelepas.
